---
title: "Job Postings Data Analysis"
author: "Nutchanon Chaiyaratana"
format:
  html: default
  docx: default
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: true
  freeze: auto
---

## Load Libraries

```{python}
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, count, avg, desc, to_date
```

## Start Spark Session

```{python}
spark = SparkSession.builder.appName("JobPostingsAnalysis").getOrCreate()
```

## Load Dataset

```{python}
df = spark.read.option("header", "true") \
    .option("inferSchema", "false") \
    .option("multiLine", "true") \
    .option("escape", "\"") \
    .csv("data/lightcast_job_postings.csv")

df.printSchema()
```

## Show First Five Rows

```{python}
df.show(5, truncate=False)
```

## Count Total Job Postings

```{python}
total_jobs = df.count()
print("Total job postings:", total_jobs)
```

## Remove Duplicates

```{python}
df_clean = df.dropDuplicates(["ID"])
print("Rows after removing duplicates:", df_clean.count())
```

## Convert Salary Columns

```{python}
df_clean = df_clean.withColumn("SALARY_FROM", col("SALARY_FROM").cast("double")) \
                   .withColumn("SALARY_TO", col("SALARY_TO").cast("double"))

df_clean = df_clean.withColumn(
    "AVG_SALARY",
    (col("SALARY_FROM") + col("SALARY_TO")) / 2
)
```

## Convert Date Columns

```{python}
df_clean = df_clean.withColumn("POSTED_DATE", to_date(col("POSTED"))) \
                   .withColumn("EXPIRED_DATE", to_date(col("EXPIRED")))
```

## Top 5 Job Titles

```{python}
top_titles = df_clean.groupBy("TITLE_CLEAN") \
    .agg(count("*").alias("job_count")) \
    .orderBy(desc("job_count")) \
    .limit(5)

top_titles.show()
```

## Average Salary by Employment Type

```{python}
salary_by_type = df_clean.groupBy("EMPLOYMENT_TYPE") \
    .agg(avg("AVG_SALARY").alias("average_salary")) \
    .orderBy(desc("average_salary"))

salary_by_type.show()
```

## State with the Most Job Postings

```{python}
state_counts = df_clean.groupBy("STATE") \
    .agg(count("*").alias("job_count")) \
    .orderBy(desc("job_count")) \
    .limit(5)

state_counts.show()
```

## Stop Spark Session

```{python}
spark.stop()
```
